codeReview.summary: The commit adds a comprehensive new Python integration test module and meaningfully exercises canonicalization, PA enrichment, EV scoring, outreach generation, and county-level error isolation. However, two required behaviors remain unproven by the implemented tests: dashboard backward compatibility is not validated through dashboard rendering, and graceful-degradation tests do not execute the actual pipeline catch-and-continue path.

issues.description: Backward-compat validation for VAL-CROSS-006 is not implemented as specified. The feature description requires verifying dashboard rendering for leads missing new fields (no EV / no scoreBreakdown) without errors, and validation-contract.md defines VAL-CROSS-006 as an agent-browser dashboard check. BackwardCompatibilityTests only call backend helpers (_score_with_breakdown, build_outreach_prompt, generate_outreach_batch) and never exercise React rendering paths or console-error behavior, so the requirement can regress while tests still pass.

Graceful-degradation tests do not verify pipeline-level continuation behavior. In both voter-failure tests (including lines 395-399 and 446-448), RuntimeError is caught directly in test code after calling the patched voter function, rather than invoking build_canonical_lead_dataset() (or equivalent pipeline orchestration path) and asserting the pipeline itself catches and continues with preserved PA enrichments. This can pass even if pipeline exception handling regresses.

sharedStateObservations.observation: There is a feature-contract mapping ambiguity around VAL-CROSS-006: the validation contract defines it as dashboard/browser verification, but features.json assigns VAL-CROSS-006 to this python-backend feature while the dashboard cross-area feature does not claim it. This mismatch likely drove backend-only implementation for a UI assertion.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: validation-contract.md:597-600 (VAL-CROSS-006 tool = agent-browser); features.json:1339-1357 (cross-area-integration-tests fulfills VAL-CROSS-006); features.json:1367-1390 (cross-area-dashboard-compat fulfills VAL-CROSS-002/003/004 only).

summary: FAIL. The new tests are substantial and useful, but they do not fully satisfy this feature contract: dashboard backward compatibility is not validated at the dashboard layer, and graceful degradation is not asserted through the real pipeline continuation path.
